content.full_text:
a
This letter is in response to your letter of January 30, 2005, requesting advice
regarding procedures for waiving a stipend in order to avoid a confliciof interest under
your local ordinance. The conflict of interest is based on the council member's receipt of
a contribution. The Commission does not provide advice when the request does not
present a question under the Actl or the question presented is purely hypothetical. (Gov.
code section 831 74; rcgulation t8329, subdivisions (b)(8)(D) and (c)(e(D) and (F), copy
enclosed.)
Fern PolrrrcAt. PRACrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street . Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-s660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
February 16,2005
Councilman Mike Preston
City of San Marcos
1 Civic Center Drive
San Marcos, CA 92069-2918
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-05-015
Dear Mr. Preston
Because the facts do not present a question under the Act and the question
presented was purely hypothetical we declined to provide assistance. You have posed an
additional three questions seeking advice as to how the waiver may be implemelted and
enforced, based on a hypothetical finding that the waiver of the stipend irrquestion would
eliminate any "material financial effect."
Because none of your questions implicate the provisions of the Political Reform
Act, and they all are hypothetical in nature, we likewise decline to answer the specific
questions posed in your letter.
I Govemment Code sections 8 1000 - gl}14. Commission rcgulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. W-05-012
Page No. 2
For your information, we have enclosed the pamphlet "Can I Voter" a guide to
preventing conflicts of interest for public officials. If you have any other quesiions
regardingthis matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
By:
Lenkeit
Enclosures
wJLjg
I:\Adviceltrs W-\05-0 I 2
Counsel, Legal Division
